_meta.disclaimer: Treaty rates shown are typical statutory withholding rates for direct portfolio investment. Actual rates depend on holding period, ownership percentage, investor type (individual vs. pension vs. mutual fund), limitation-on-benefits tests, and other factors. Consult a qualified tax professional for your specific situation.

primary_sources.IRS_P901: https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/p901.pdf — IRS Publication 901: U.S. Tax Treaties (Table 1: Tax Rates on Income Other Than Personal Service Income)
primary_sources.KPMG_WHT_Guide: https://kpmg.com — KPMG annual withholding tax rate guide

state_flags.verified: Rate confirmed against a primary source cited in cell.source_citation.
state_flags.derived: Rate inferred from a primary source but requires operator double-check before relying on it.
state_flags.needs_research: Rate not yet populated. Page shows 'data pending verification' message; calculator falls back to statutory non-treaty rate.

countries.name: United States
countries.resident_note: U.S. persons taxed on worldwide dividend income; qualified dividends at 0/15/20%; ordinary dividends at ordinary rates; foreign tax credit may offset foreign withholding.

countries.name: United Kingdom
countries.resident_note: UK generally imposes no withholding on dividends paid to non-residents; UK residents taxed on foreign dividends at dividend rates with Foreign Tax Credit Relief for foreign withholding.

countries.name: Canada
countries.resident_note: Canadian residents taxed on foreign dividends as ordinary income; foreign tax credit available up to treaty rate.

countries.name: Australia
countries.resident_note: Australian residents include foreign dividends in assessable income; foreign income tax offset (FITO) available.

countries.name: Germany
countries.resident_note: 26.375% = 25% plus 5.5% solidarity surcharge. German residents taxed under final withholding (Abgeltungsteuer); foreign tax credit up to treaty rate.

countries.name: France
countries.resident_note: French residents: 30% flat tax (PFU) on dividends; option for progressive rate with foreign tax credit.

countries.name: Netherlands
countries.resident_note: Dutch residents: Box 3 wealth tax on investments; foreign tax credit for treaty withholding.

countries.name: Japan
countries.resident_note: 20.42% = 20% plus 2.1% reconstruction surtax. Japanese residents can claim foreign tax credit up to treaty rate.

countries.name: Ireland
countries.resident_note: Irish residents taxed on worldwide income; foreign tax credit available; note Ireland is a major fund-domicile for ETFs so this field also serves the many investors who hold Ireland-domiciled ETFs.

countries.name: Singapore
countries.resident_note: Singapore does not impose withholding tax on dividends to non-residents; Singapore residents are not taxed on foreign-source dividends received outside Singapore (subject to conditions).

countries.name: Switzerland
countries.resident_note: 35% statutory rate is among the highest globally but treaty rates are typically 15%. Swiss residents receive full credit for the Swiss 35% via tax return.

countries.name: Denmark
countries.resident_note: Danish residents taxed on worldwide dividend income; share income tax 27% up to threshold, 42% above.

countries.name: Sweden
countries.resident_note: Swedish residents: 30% flat tax on capital income including dividends; foreign tax credit up to treaty rate.

countries.name: Norway
countries.resident_note: Norwegian residents: dividends taxed as general income with shareholder model adjustments.

countries.name: Finland
countries.resident_note: Finnish residents: capital income tax 30% up to threshold, 34% above.

countries.name: Italy
countries.resident_note: Italian residents: 26% flat tax on dividends; foreign tax credit up to treaty rate.

countries.name: Spain
countries.resident_note: Spanish residents: savings income tax 19-28% depending on amount; foreign tax credit available.

countries.name: Belgium
countries.resident_note: Belgian residents: 30% withholding is typically final for dividends; foreign tax credit available under treaties.

countries.name: Luxembourg
countries.resident_note: Luxembourg residents taxed on worldwide income; common fund-domicile jurisdiction (SICAV/SICAF structures).

countries.name: New Zealand
countries.resident_note: New Zealand residents taxed on worldwide income; foreign investment fund (FIF) rules may apply; foreign tax credit available.

treaties.source_citation: IRS Publication 901 Table 1 (United Kingdom row, Dividends Paid by U.S. Corporations column)
treaties.notes: 15% on ordinary portfolio dividends. Pension funds may qualify for 0% under Article 10(3). Treaty must be claimed on Form W-8BEN.

treaties.source_citation: IRS Publication 901 Table 1 (Canada row, Dividends column)
treaties.notes: 15% on direct portfolio investment. 5% available to corporate shareholders with ≥10% ownership.

treaties.treaty_reference: 1989 US-Germany Tax Treaty (as amended 2006 Protocol), Article 10 (Dividends)
treaties.source_citation: IRS Publication 901 Table 1 (Germany row, Dividends column)
treaties.notes: 15% on portfolio dividends. Germany's statutory 26.375% is reduced via refund claim (Erstattung) or at-source certification.

treaties.source_citation: IRS Publication 901 Table 1 (Netherlands row, Dividends column)
IRS Publication 901 Table 1 (Switzerland row, Dividends column)
treaties.notes: 15% on portfolio dividends. Switzerland's statutory 35% is reduced via refund (Form 82 series) or at-source with a qualified intermediary.

treaties.treaty_reference: 2003 US-Japan Tax Treaty (as amended 2013 Protocol), Article 10
treaties.source_citation: IRS Publication 901 Table 1 (Japan row, Dividends column)
treaties.notes: 10% on portfolio dividends; 0% for pension funds under Article 10(3).

treaties.treaty_reference: 1982 US-Australia Tax Treaty (as amended 2001 Protocol), Article 10
treaties.source_citation: IRS Publication 901 Table 1 (Australia row, Dividends column)
treaties.notes: 15% on franked or unfranked portfolio dividends.

treaties.source_citation: IRS Publication 901 Table 1 (Ireland row, Dividends column)
treaties.notes: 15% on portfolio dividends. Ireland is also a major fund-domicile jurisdiction; Irish-domiciled ETFs are subject to separate fund-level tax mechanics.

treaties.treaty_reference: 1994 US-France Tax Treaty (as amended 2009 Protocol), Article 10
treaties.source_citation: IRS Publication 901 Table 1 (France row, Dividends column)
IRC §§ 1441–1446 (withholding applies to foreign persons; US residents not subject to WHT on US-source dividends)
treaties.notes: U.S. residents receiving U.S.-source dividends: no cross-border withholding; taxed on return at qualified (0/15/20%) or ordinary rates.
